div1.entryFree:
Bleak, 1) cold, chill: thou liest in the b. air, As II, 6, 16. to make his b. winds kiss my parched lips, John V, 7, 40. Tim. IV, 3, 222. Lr. II, 4, 303 (Ff high).
2) exposed to the cold, open to the cold wind: our lodgings, standing b. upon the sea, Per. III, 2, 14.
3) pale with frost, chilled: look b. in the cold wind, All's I, 1, 115.
